description_long: This object depicts a small rural farmstead consisting of two main residential buildings surrounded by fenced yards and intersecting gravel roads. The larger building features a gabled roof, a prominent brick chimney, and a covered porch, while the smaller building has a simpler design with a steep roof and a single entrance. The property is enclosed by wooden fencing and includes a small outbuilding or shed adjacent to the main house, as well as some scattered hay bales. The ground is covered with a grassy texture, and the roads are clearly demarcated, forming a crossroad at the center of the scene. The overall layout suggests a traditional, early 20th-century American rural homestead.
description: A rural farmstead with two gabled residential buildings, fenced yards, a shed, and intersecting gravel roads on grassy terrain.
description_view_0: Front view showing both main houses, fenced yards, and the central crossroad.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the back of the main house, the shed, and hay bales.
description_view_2: Side view with a clear look at the porch of the main house and the smaller house across the road.
description_view_3: Opposite side view focusing on the back of the smaller house and the side of the main house.
